response:
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"

SECTION 1  
An innocent person, having been wrongly judged, might express disbelief and confusion. They could say, "This can’t be right—how could they lead me to this conclusion?" The emotional weight of the moment might leave them speechless, but eventually, they might try to clarify their position. "I never did anything to deserve this," they might say, struggling to process the injustice. A stranger walking by might notice their distress and offer a word of comfort.

SECTION 2  
The innocent person might feel a mix of anger and helplessness, but also a desire to prove their innocence. They could say, "I have a silver lining in my story—proof that I was wronged." If given the chance to speak, they might address the court directly, saying, "Sir, I beg you to reconsider. I didn’t do this." They might even ask for time to wrap up their case, hoping to present the truth. A stranger’s presence could remind them that they are not alone in this fight.
